event_world_42_20260423_085102_0000.name: Jazz Under the Stars
event_world_42_20260423_085102_0000.description: Grammy-winning trumpeter Marcus Cole leads a quartet through classic Coltrane, Miles Davis, and original compositions beneath an open sky.

reviews.text: The event started ninety minutes late with no explanation or apology from the organisers.
Electric atmosphere from start to finish — the performance was world-class.
Beautifully curated programme with a strong balance of emerging and established artists.

event_world_42_20260423_085102_0001.name: Wine & Cheese Masterclass
event_world_42_20260423_085102_0001.description: A Michelin-starred sommelier guides guests through rare bottles paired with artisan cheeses, revealing the science and art behind perfect flavor harmony.

reviews.text: Middle-of-the-road experience — worth attending once but not something to travel specifically for.

Great selection of local craft vendors; the live folk music added a lovely ambient backdrop.
Parking chaos added two hours to our evening and the toilet facilities were disgraceful.

event_world_42_20260423_085102_0002.name: International Stand-Up Night
event_world_42_20260423_085102_0002.description: Six comedians from six continents take the stage for a two-hour showcase of wildly different humour styles — slapstick, dark comedy, and sharp political satire.

reviews.text: Average experience — neither the worst nor the best event I have attended this year.
One of those rare events that exceeds every expectation — unforgettable performances all round.
Decent event overall though the merchandise stall ran out of programmes by midday.
Ticket prices do not reflect the actual production values — feels like a premium charge for a mid-tier experience.
Terrible organisation — queues stretched around the block and we missed the first act entirely.
Average experience — neither the worst nor the best event I have attended this year.
Too many commercial sponsors with their logos dominating every surface — completely killed the atmosphere.

event_world_42_20260423_085102_0003.name: Ramen Showdown
event_world_42_20260423_085102_0003.description: Ten ramen chefs compete head-to-head for the title of best bowl in the city. Guests vote, judges deliberate, and everyone leaves full.

reviews.text: Average experience — neither the worst nor the best event I have attended this year.
Accessible entrances were well signed and staff were genuinely helpful to our group member using a wheelchair.
Long waits at every food and drink stall; the cashless payment system crashed repeatedly.
Overcrowded beyond safe limits; I had to leave early as the crowd pressure near the stage felt dangerous.
Solid community event with a genuine local flavour — not flashy but heartfelt.

Decent event overall though the merchandise stall ran out of programmes by midday.
